examples: Of wet alpine and subalpine meadows of Canada and Alaska.
It has many types of ecosystems, but the subalpine forest is most abundant.
As you gain elevation, you leave the montane areas and enter the subalpine ecosystem.
The subalpine forests are dominated by lodgepole pine (Pinus contorta), P. albicaulis and alpine fir (Abies lasiocarpa).
Extensive parkland with a mosaic of forest patches and subalpine meadow communities extends from 1, 500 - 2, 000 meters above sea level.
